## **Who We Are** The UC Davis Art/Science Fusion Program (housed in the Science and Society Program, SAS) is an exciting collaboration among design faculty, science faculty, museum educators, professional artists, UC Davis students and community members.  The program revolves around a teaching and learning model in which lectures or workshops present concepts and site-specific contexts (scientific, artistic, cultural and their interfaces) and hands-on experience with artistic mediums (e.g. ceramics, painting, textile surface design, photography, performing arts, song writing) are the forum for participant expression and extension of learning.  Founded by UC Davis Entomology Professor Diane Ullman and nationally-known ceramicist Donna Billick, the program offers UC Davis students, K-12 students and diverse members of the community the opportunity to learn and practice art and science in an integrated, hands-on, project-based setting.  Starting in Fall 2000, Ullman began offering Entomology 1, Art, Science and the World of Insects in an intellectual and technological partnership with Billick. The course became the centerpiece and inspiration for the Art/Science Fusion Program (formed in 2006 with pilot funding from UC Davis Provost Hinshaw). Ullman and Billick now serve as Co-Founder and Co-Directors of the program.  As the program evolved and garnered institutional attention, the Science and Society Program became our institutional home; faculty partners stepped forward and proposed new undergraduate courses (Professors Terrence R. Nathan and Wendy Silk, environmental sciences faculty) and colleagues in the Design Department (Gale Okumura) guided independent study design classes to produce graphic documentation.  Strong graduate student mentoring; a community element (in partnership with the UC Davis Arboretum); and, a K-12 education outreach component emerged.  Exciting collaborations were born with artists, designers, museum educators, and scientists (e.g. Catherine Chalmers, Ann Savageau and James Housefield).  With the Art/Science Fusion Program as a catalyst, many special undergraduate seminar classes, a national exhibit, community workshops and a model K-12 program have been developed that extend the Art/Science Fusion Program’s collaborative learning pedagogy.

![Picture](/uploads/2/4/2/5/24253915/2473662.gif?1385066221)

# Art and Science Fusion Program

Where science meets creativity—help us support the Art/Science Fusion Program!

The Art and Science Fusion Program is a one-of-a-kind experience that brings together the worlds of entomology and art to spark curiosity, creativity, and scientific understanding. By blending hands-on learning with artistic expression, this innovative program helps students from all majors connect with the fascinating world of insects—exploring their biology, ecological roles, and cultural significance. It not only enhances scientific literacy in an engaging and memorable way, but also opens up unique career and educational pathways in both science and the arts. Your support helps keep this powerful, interdisciplinary learning experience thriving for the next generation of students.

enriched_points.expanded_content: The UC Davis Art-Science Fusion Program integrates artistic mediums with scientific concepts through hands-on projects, fostering creativity and curiosity about entomology and ecological roles.
This program actively engages K-12 students, university students, and community members, creating a collaborative environment that enhances learning experiences and promotes scientific literacy.

Participants benefit from an innovative, interdisciplinary approach that improves understanding of scientific concepts while developing artistic skills, ultimately preparing them for diverse career paths.
